channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why Operation Epic Fury Is Catastrophic for Xi
item.description: The U.S.–Israeli military campaign has created palpable problems for China.

item.title: Early voting on Virginia's new congressional map gets green light as judge rejects challenge
item.description: Early voting on Virginia's congressional redistricting referendum will begin Friday after a judge dismissed a lawsuit that sought to delay the vote ov...

item.title: The Hurdles to Democracy in Iran
item.description: And how to potentially overcome them.

item.title: Uber CEO says his ‘really demanding’ work culture includes expecting employees to answer his emails over the weekend: ‘Don’t come here if you want to coast’
item.description: Uber boss Dara Khosrowshahi says the most important skill in life is working hard: “I’m not going to let anyone outwork me.”

item.title: Trump’s $175 billion illegal tariff revenue is now accruing interest, and refund delays could be costing American taxpayers $700 million a month
item.description: “Consumers will be the biggest losers here,” a Cato Institute economist told Fortune.

item.title: Republicans hand Trump the wheel on Iran — but one red line emerges
item.description: GOP lawmakers signal support for ongoing missile strikes in Iran while drawing a firm red line at ground troops, insisting on Congress' constitutional...

item.title: Texas Rep. Julie Johnson and Colin Allred advance to runoff in Democratic primary for U.S. House
item.description: Texas Rep. Julie Johnson and former Rep. Colin Allred have advanced to runoff in a Democratic primary for U.S. House.

item.title: SCOTUS Rules That Courts Should Defer To Immigration Judges On Asylum Cases
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court unanimously sided with the Trump administration on Wednesday in a dispute over the standard for reviewing the cases of illegal ...

item.title: ‘We Are Just Getting Started’: Hegseth Signals Prolonged U.S. Commitment In Iran
item.description: Questions that remains unanswered are who will take control of Iran after all of its potential leaders or successors are dead, as Hegseth said? What w...

item.title: Ella McCay — Hollywood’s Definitive Disaster
item.description: James L. Brooks loses his sense of humor to politics.

item.title: The Texas Primaries Just Ended A Bunch of Jerry Springer Episodes
item.description: The primary elections in Texas this week may bring us closer to the daily heartburn of a Sen. James Talarico, endlessly sermonizing on the biblical ma...

item.title: IRS leader Bisignano declines to answer questions over unlawful taxpayer data disclosures to ICE
item.description: The head of the IRS largely declined to answer questions about recent unlawful disclosures of taxpayer data when he was questioned by lawmakers at a c...

item.title: The average American teacher makes $72,000, but one in three are so broke they’re taking on side hustles like Uber driving
item.description: Teaching is seen as an AI-proof career by Gen Z—but with the American Dream becoming more out of reach, educators have turned to second jobs to make e...

item.title: For Media, The Only Good Christian Is A Fake One Like James Talarico
item.description: When Democrats conceal radical left-wing ideology in Christian language, media celebrate it as a savvy communications strategy and describe it as 'hea...

item.title: Leavitt says ground troops in Iran not currently being considered, doesn't rule it out
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt won't rule out sending ground troops to Iran as part of Operation Epic Fury but says they are "not part o...

item.title: What's next for embattled Rep. Tony Gonzales after being forced into runoff?
item.description: Rep. Tony Gonzales survived Tuesday's GOP primary -- but just barely. Now, with a runoff looming, a congressional ethics investigation underway and mo...

item.title: Viral deepfake ad casts Musk, Bezos, and Altman as corpulent overlords powering AI on human sweat. Its creator says the best jokes tell the truth
item.description: The viral AI ad imagines laid-off workers pedaling bikes to power the technology that replaced them.

item.title: California Told Us to Deceive Parents, but We Said No
item.description: Teachers shouldn’t be forced to lie to parents about their child’s asserted gender identity.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll Do ‘Right Thing’ In Iran No Matter The Polls. Let’s See The Same Approach To Deportations
item.description: The mass deportation of illegal immigrants is every bit as righteous and 'the right thing' to do as Trump claims bombing Iran is.

item.title: Goldman’s top strategist warns stocks are flashing the same warning signs as before the 2008 financial crisis
item.description: Peter Oppenheimer—who correctly called U.S. underperformance and the global rotation—now sees elevated risk of a correction.

item.title: Biden-appointed judge threatens jail for ICE officials in clash over Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: A Minnesota judge appointed by former President Joe Biden threatened he may rule to imprison ICE and DOJ officials during a fiery courtroom exchange w...

item.title: Trump teases kingmaker endorsement in Texas ‘soon’ to force other candidate out of runoff
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn, R-Texas, and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton are headed for a bloody runoff, but President Donald Trump wants to end things quickl...

item.title: Congress Had Plenty of Opportunities to Stop the Iran Strikes
item.description: Despite what his congressional critics say, Trump’s actions in Iran are in line with the Framers’ views of presidential power.

item.title: Battle for Texas Senate seat hinges on Republican runoff and a Trump endorsement
item.description: The fate of a U.S. Senate seat in Texas likely rests on a May 26 GOP runoff between four-term Republican incumbent John Cornyn and the state's ultra-c...

item.title: Number of Iranian ballistic missiles fired plunges since start of Operation Epic Fury
item.description: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs Gen. Dan Caine said Iranian missile and drone launches have sharply declined since Operation Epic Fury began.

item.title: International Women’s Day: Women Leaders Put Nutrition First
item.description: Women are three times more likely than men to invest in eating well. The post International Women’s Day: Women Leaders Put Nutrition First appeared fi...

item.title: NLP and Yield Curve Prediction From Central Bank Minutes
item.description: Can algorithms that analyze central bank language help predict the next move in the yield curve? The post NLP and Yield Curve Prediction From Central ...

item.title: Three Levers That Drive VC Returns
item.description: VC performance hinges on ownership and failure reduction, not just rare breakout wins. The post Three Levers That Drive VC Returns appeared first on C...

item.title: Geopolitical Risk and Portfolio Oversight
item.description: A framework for identifying material geopolitical shocks, translating them into portfolio impacts, and documenting risk for oversight and governance. ...

item.title: Aligning Allocation to the Global Business Cycle
item.description: Static portfolios lag macro shifts. Predefined cycle triggers help practitioners adjust risk before markets reprice. The post Aligning Allocation to t...
